ex_test_001.note: 3-task plan (RESEARCHER + 2xCODER). Clear goal, named output files (watcher.py, parquet_writer.py, cli.py), API contract consistent (append_to_parquet named in both CODER tasks), specific API references (watchdog FileSystemEventHandler, on_created, pandas to_parquet, click.command). No hallucinated APIs. Valid decomposition of a realistic Python CLI goal.

ex_test_002.note: 3-task plan (2xCODER + UIDEVELOPER). TheOrc WPF domain example — exemplary for CODER/UIDEVELOPER split. API contract explicitly maintained: SwarmTaskViewModel, ObservableCollection<SwarmTaskViewModel>.Tasks, and ElapsedDisplay property all named consistently across all three task descriptions. Deep descriptions with specific WPF types (INotifyPropertyChanged, DispatcherTimer, DataTrigger). No hallucinated APIs.

ex_test_003.note: Single-task collapse: goal is too vague ('Build a file renamer tool.'), only 1 task, description is 12 chars ('Rename files.'). No training signal. Score 65 is in the marginal 40-69 range. Test fixture — not from a real swarm run. Neither useful as positive training nor as a clean negative example.

ex_test_004.note: 2-task plan (RESEARCHER + CODER). 2 tasks appropriate for this scope — single output module, no UI. Ollama domain directly relevant to TheOrc's stack. Specific API references (FastAPI @app.post, SummarizeRequest model, async httpx.AsyncClient, /api/generate). Named output file (api.py). Loses gold only because 2 tasks limits decomposition training signal.
